instructions: A file named input.txt exists in the working directory. Write a program solve.py that prints, to stdout, the number of NON-BLANK lines in input.txt (lines that contain at least one non-whitespace character). Print just the integer.
A CSV file data.csv with a header row exists in the working directory. Write solve.py that prints, to stdout, the integer sum of the 'amount' column (values are integers). Print just the integer.
A file items.txt exists with one item per line (possibly with duplicates and surrounding whitespace). Write solve.py that prints the unique items, trimmed, sorted ascending, one per line.

A file text.txt exists. Write solve.py that prints the single most frequent word in text.txt. Words are maximal runs of ASCII letters, compared case-insensitively and printed in lowercase. If tied, print the one that is smallest alphabetically.

files.text.txt: The fox and the dog. The FOX ran; the fox WON.

instructions: A file config.ini exists with lines of the form key=value. Write solve.py that reads a key name from standard input (a single line) and prints the corresponding value. If the key is absent, print MISSING.
